name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is a leading enterprise cloud platform company that provides AI-powered workflow automation, IT service management, and digital transformation solutions. Nearly nine in ten Fortune 500 companies use its platform, which boasts a 98% renewal rate.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: ServiceNow's Now Assist AI offering surpassed $600M in ACV with net new ACV more than doubling YoY in Q4 2025, nearly tripling deals above $1M quarter-over-quarter [1]. The company co-developed the Apriel Nemotron 15B reasoning model with NVIDIA, introduced AI Control Tower for centralized AI agent management, and launched AI Agent Fabric for cross-platform agent orchestration [2]. Strategic acquisitions of Armis (cybersecurity) and Veza (identity security) expand the platform into adjacent categories. Partnerships with both Anthropic and OpenAI ensure model-agnostic AI capabilities. ServiceNow started building LLMs into its platform six years before the agentic AI wave, demonstrating prescient R&D investment.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: CEO Bill McDermott's 'people before profits' philosophy is backed by action — all 28,000 employees have undergone AI skill assessments with personalized reskilling programs, demonstrating genuine commitment to workforce development rather than displacement [2]. McDermott's leadership narrative — from buying a deli at 16 to leading the fastest-growing organic enterprise software company in history — embodies an aspirational mission. The 'hungry and humble' culture emphasizes continuous improvement without complacency. The company's 98% renewal rate and 25% growth in monthly active users suggest strong customer and employee alignment with the mission.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: ServiceNow delivered $13.28B in FY2025 revenue growing 20.7% YoY, with subscription revenue at 21% growth and non-GAAP operating margin of 31% [1]. The balance sheet is pristine with D/E of just 18.5%, ROE of 15.5%, and free cash flow margin of 36% projected for 2026. Forward P/E of 22.1x is remarkably reasonable for a company growing 21% with 98% gross retention. The 5yr revenue CAGR of ~25% reflects sustained hypergrowth. The $5B share repurchase authorization signals confidence in intrinsic value. Liquidity is exceptional at $2B+ daily traded value.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI is ServiceNow's primary growth engine, not a threat — Now Assist ACV surpassed $600M with a clear path to $1B+ in 2026, and AI drove 244 deals over $1M in net new ACV (up 40% YoY) [1]. The platform's workflow automation foundation is uniquely positioned for the agentic AI era, as AI agents need orchestration infrastructure that ServiceNow already provides. CEO McDermott argues that AI is 'eating features and functions companies' while benefiting platform companies that connect modernized workflows with data [2]. The Apriel Nemotron 15B model, AI Control Tower, and AI Agent Fabric represent first-mover advantages in enterprise AI agent management. The opportunity tiebreaker is fully earned — ServiceNow is a primary AI beneficiary.

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under McDermott exemplifies infinite game thinking through its platform-first strategy, people-centric AI approach, and relentless focus on building long-term enterprise relationships over short-term revenue maximization.

just_cause.analysis: ServiceNow's just cause — making work more productive and rewarding through AI-powered workflow automation — is compelling and enduring. McDermott's framing of AI as 'civilization's greatest opportunity' that should 'work for people' elevates the mission beyond enterprise software to societal impact [2]. The company's $27M+ in social investment and 27% employee volunteer participation rate add community dimension. The mission clearly outlasts any individual leader and grows more relevant as AI reshapes work.

trusting_teams.analysis: McDermott's decision to provide all 28,000 employees with AI skill assessments and personalized reskilling — rather than letting AI anxiety fester — is a masterclass in trust-building [2]. His philosophy that 'employees won't be loyal if they're worried AI will destroy their lives' demonstrates empathetic leadership. The 'hungry and humble' culture empowers employees to push boundaries without fear of failure. McDermott's warnings against leaders who 'over-control' and his belief that 'great leaders set the standard, trust their people, and remove roadblocks' reflect genuine distributed trust.

worthy_rivals.analysis: ServiceNow openly competes with Salesforce, Microsoft, and emerging AI-native platforms, and McDermott's competitive framing — 'legacy enterprise systems multiplied like rabbits' — shows he uses rivals' weaknesses as strategic opportunities rather than dismissing them. The simultaneous partnerships with both Anthropic and OpenAI demonstrate willingness to embrace rival AI providers rather than picking a single winner.

existential_flexibility.analysis: ServiceNow's pivot from IT service management to an AI-powered enterprise platform represents significant strategic evolution — starting to build LLMs into the platform six years before the agentic AI boom showed prescience [1]. The Armis and Veza acquisitions expand into cybersecurity, demonstrating willingness to evolve the platform's scope. However, the core platform model has remained consistent since McDermott's arrival, representing more of an expansion than a reinvention.

courage_to_lead.analysis: McDermott showed courage in declaring that AI agents and workflows are 'harmonious and synonymous' — a bold positioning bet in a market where many fear AI will displace workflow software [2]. The $5B buyback authorization during a 31% stock decline shows conviction. However, the 2026 guidance of 20.5-21% growth, while strong, reflects continuation rather than bold reinvention. The AI investments, while significant, follow rather than lead market trends.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the dominant enterprise AI workflow platform, with Now Assist surpassing $600M ACV (doubling YoY) on a path to $1B+ in 2026, driving 21% subscription revenue growth at 31% non-GAAP operating margins [1]. The company's first-mover advantage in AI agent orchestration — through AI Control Tower and AI Agent Fabric — positions it as the 'operating system' for enterprise AI deployments across nearly 90% of the Fortune 500. At forward P/E of 22.1x with 20%+ growth and 36% FCF margins, the risk/reward is compelling following a 31% stock decline. The $5B buyback authorization and pristine balance sheet (D/E 18.5%) provide downside protection [1][2].
thesis_continued: The bear case rests on valuation compression risk if AI disruption fears materialize — the 31% stock decline reflects market anxiety that AI agents could displace traditional workflow software, not just augment it [1]. Trailing P/E of 66x creates vulnerability to any growth deceleration. Profit margins of 13.2% on a GAAP basis (vs 31% non-GAAP) reveal significant stock-based compensation dilution that flatters reported profitability. The Armis acquisition's expected H2 2026 close introduces integration risk and dilutes the platform's focus. Competition from Microsoft's Copilot, Salesforce's Agentforce, and AI-native startups is intensifying across ServiceNow's core workflow automation market.
key_catalysts: Now Assist ACV targeting $1B+ in 2026 (from $600M), with AI deals above $1M nearly tripling QoQ — a clear inflection in enterprise AI monetization
AI Agent Fabric and AI Control Tower establish ServiceNow as the orchestration layer for multi-vendor AI agent deployments across the enterprise
FY2026 guidance of 20.5-21% subscription revenue growth at 32% non-GAAP operating margin with 36% FCF margin demonstrates profitable hypergrowth
key_risks: AI disruption fear has driven a 31% stock decline — if AI agents can replace workflow automation (not just augment it), ServiceNow's value proposition could erode
GAAP profit margin of 13.2% vs 31% non-GAAP reveals heavy stock-based compensation that dilutes existing shareholders by ~18 percentage points
Intensifying competition from Microsoft Copilot, Salesforce Agentforce, and AI-native platforms threatens ServiceNow's enterprise workflow dominance
sources: ServiceNow Q4/FY2025 Earnings Release — $13.28B revenue, $600M Now Assist ACV, January 2026
